what_it_does: This file is a ledger of extracted color-palette findings across multiple pitch-deck and landing-page design substrates; each entry records a color usage pattern, a recipe, supporting evidence, an emergent flag, a generator rule, and the source deck plus compression depth where it was observed.
observations: Entries are almost all in the 'color-palette' category.
Sources span capital-raise, company-info, deck1-2026, pitch-deck, presentation-15p, product-vi-landing, product-vt, and recent-pitches.
A warm gold/amber accent is described repeatedly, but with varying hex values across sources.
Near-black warm text and warm cream/off-white backgrounds are consistently prescribed.
Rare colors (maroon, green, red, orange-amber) are reserved for specific semantic contexts.
Some entries include pixel-sampled measurements with sample sizes; others are eyeballed.
Several entries explicitly forbid pure black, pure white, or pure yellow.
Background rules differ: some sources say never use #FFFFFF, others list #ffffff as the default slide background.
Red and green are treated as directional-only semantic interrupts, not general accents.
standouts: A persistent warm-gold mono-accent system is the dominant signal across all substrates.
Rare colors are treated as semantic signals precisely because they are scarce.
Measured pixel-sampled color values are recorded alongside generator prescriptions.
Explicit negative rules ('never use pure black', 'never use gold for body text') appear throughout.
Background hierarchy is described as white → cream → hatched-cream encoding ambient → contained → emphatic data zones.
conventions: Findings follow a consistent schema: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent, generator_rule, _src, _depth.
Color descriptions mix approximate hex, HSL, RGB, and verbal characterizations.
Each entry ties a pattern to a specific source deck and compression depth.
Generator rules are prescriptive and often use 'never'/'only' phrasing.
The file breaks internal consistency by reporting slightly different gold hex values and conflicting background-color rules across sources.
concerns: Multiple similar entries report conflicting hex ranges for the same 'brand gold' concept.
Background-color guidance is inconsistent: some entries forbid pure white while others prescribe it.
'Emergent' classification is not defined or justified within the file.
Evidence references page numbers without accompanying image or slide identifiers.
There is no timestamp, version, or canonical-token reconciliation mechanism.
notes: A cross-substrate palette rulebook that attempts to stabilize a warm-gold, near-black, cream design language while recording where rare colors carry specific semantic meaning.
questions: Which gold hex value is canonical given the reported range?
How are conflicts between measured JPEG medians and CSS source values resolved?
What criteria determine whether a finding is marked 'emergent'?
Are page-number references consistent across different deck versions?
Is there a unified token naming scheme intended for downstream code?
What is the intended consumer of these generator rules?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended as an interpretive findings ledger and rule reference for a shared color language; it does document color patterns and generator rules, so its purpose largely matches its content, though it also functions as a raw conflict set.
apparent_themes: Directional semantics for red and green
intention_signals: It is trying to extract, codify, and preserve a shared color-language across many deck and landing-page variants so that future generated designs can reuse consistent tokens and rare colors retain precise, non-decorative meaning.
novelty: It treats color rarity itself as a semantic signal and explicitly forbids decorative use of rare colors; it also records pixel-sampled median values alongside generator rules.
contribution: Provides a structured set of color usage recipes and generator rules tied to specific source decks and compression depths, including semantic restrictions and measured color samples, which a fused system could use to derive canonical tokens and preserve intentional color meaning.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON array of color-palette findings extracted from multiple pitch-deck and landing-page substrates, documenting warm-gold accent usage, near-black text, cream backgrounds, rare semantic colors, and generator rules per source and compression depth.
intention_for_embedding: To capture and standardize the color-language rules and emergent semantic patterns across a family of investor and product design artifacts.
